message:
fix(fr-runtime): ACL DRYRUN denial returns BulkString with upstream wording (partial br-frankenredis-faqe)

Upstream acl.c::aclCommand DRYRUN emits the denial message as a
BulkString via addReplyBulkSds; our runtime was returning a RESP
Error. The phrasing also needed adjustment: upstream uses
`User <name> has no permissions to ...` (no quotes around the
user name, lowercase cmd fullname) as produced by
getAclErrorMessage, whereas our version prefixed `ERR User '<n>'`.

- fr-runtime DRYRUN: return BulkString for Command/Key/Channel
  denials; message now matches upstream getAclErrorMessage
- fr-runtime unit tests: update the 7 asserts that expected
  `RespFrame::Error(...no permissions...)` to match BulkString
- core_acl.json: flip 5 dryrun-denial fixture expectations from
  `kind: error` to `kind: bulk` with upstream wording
- remove 5 dryrun-denial XFAILs from live-oracle ACL test

fr-runtime 275 passed; fr-command 639 passed;
fr-conformance 180 passed, 0 failed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
